October 16, 1981 
Statements of Economic IntPrf'o;f 
322 6444 
Patricia Murray, City Clerk, City of Fontana, has 
forwarded your Statement of Economic Interests to this office 
along with a request for advice on your behalf. 
According to 
Ms. Murray, you have objected to completing Schedu1e H of your 
Form 721, because you feel that disclosure of the names of 
your customers would be a violation of their privacy rights. 
Section 87207(b) of the Government Code provides that you must 
report the names of all customers of any business in which you 
own a ten percent or greater share, if your pro rata share of 
the gross receipts from the customer is $10,000.00 or more 
during a calendar year. 
It is this provision which you claim 
vioJates your customers' rights of privacy. 
The California Supreme Court has considered the question 
of whether the financial disclosure provisions of the Political 
Reform Act violate the constitutional rights of privacy of per-
sons filing statements, or of persons or businesses whose names 
must be listed on statements as sources of income to the filers 
of those statements. 
The court has held that such disclosure 
may be required under the Political Reform Act, without violating 
constitutional riqht~). 
See Hayes v. Wood, (1979) 25 Ca1.3d 772; 
Count 
of Nevada v. !vkMillen,(l974) 11 Ca1.3d 662. 
Therefore 
excuse you-from-Th-e requirement of Section 87207 of 
and must require you to file Schedule H. 
Enclosed is a 
:~unplement:::ll Schedule 721-H, Income to 
Business Entities, Wllich should be completed and sent to the 
City Clerk who wi 11 reL.dn a copy and forward the original to 
the Commissiun. 


Lawrence M. Moniak 
October 16, 1981 
Page Two 
With regard to your city clerk's request.for waiver of 
a fine for late filing your Statement of Economic Interests 
which was due April I, 1981, and according to guidelines 
approved by the Fair Political Practices Commission and in 
light of all information available to the staff, your fine 
is hereby waived. 
The Commission expects that you will make 
every effort to file promptly in the future. 
pleasE-' call me at (916) 322-6444 if you have any 
questions regarding this matter.

Enclosures 
cc: 
City Clerk 
Sincerely, 
Alice Hughes 
Documents Examiner

sections.question:
s regarding this matter. 
AH: jp 
Enclosures 
cc: 
City Clerk
sections.conclusion: Section 87207(b) requires reporting of customer names when the filer owns a ten percent or greater share of a business and the pro rata share of gross receipts from the customer is $10,000 or more during a calendar year. The California Supreme Court has held that such disclosure may be required under the Political Reform Act without violating constitutional rights. Therefore, the Commission cannot excuse the filer from the requirement of Section 87207 and must require completion of Schedule H. Additionally, the fine for late filing the Statement of Economic Interests is waived.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the requirement to disclose customer names on Schedule H of Form 721 violate constitutional privacy rights, and can a Planning Commissioner be excused from this disclosure requirement?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC holds that disclosure of customer names under Section 87207(b) does not violate constitutional privacy rights based on California Supreme Court precedent, and therefore the filer must complete Schedule H. The Commission waives the late filing fine while expecting prompt future filings.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document is a clear advice letter responding to a request forwarded by City Clerk. Contains explicit Question and Conclusion sections. References to Hayes v. Wood and County of Nevada v. McMillen cases. Minor OCR artifacts present ('Califorl/la', 'Commissiol/') but content is legible and complete.

CONCLUSION: Section 87207(b) requires reporting of customer names when the filer owns a ten percent or greater share of a business and the pro rata share of gross receipts from the customer is $10,000 or more during a calendar year. The California Supreme Court has held that such disclosure may be required under the Political Reform Act without violating constitutional rights. Therefore, the Commission cannot excuse the filer from the requirement of Section 87207 and must require completion of Schedule H. Additionally, the fine for late filing the Statement of Economic Interests is waived.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises Lawrence M. Moniak, a Planning Commissioner, that he must complete Schedule H of his Form 721 to disclose customer names despite privacy concerns, as required by law and upheld by California courts. The Commission also waives his late filing fine.
